content_hints.description: The source text does not contain any information regarding 'dragon_heist - Manshoon Sanctum'.
content_hints.read_aloud: > You see a section of the basement wall pivot inward at your touch, revealing a staircase cut from black stone that swallows the light. Each step is engraved with a symbol that glows faintly as you place your weight on it—a slow, descending sequence that tugs at the edge of your understanding. The air grows colder with every step, and by the time you reach the bottom, something has settled in the back of your mind that was not there before.

hazards.name: Psychic resonance (Wisdom save or unsettling visions)

event_triggers: On entry: The door seals behind you. No sound passes through it in either direction.

content_hints.description: The Cassalanters’ private library is filled with the scent of old parchment, and its walls are lined with tall shelves packed with colorful, leather-bound books. A secret compartment in the base of a shelf can be detected and opened with a successful DC 15 Wisdom (Perception) check, revealing Duhlark’s wand of binding hidden inside.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a wrestling mat sprawled on the floor in the middle of the room, surrounded by velvet armchairs, small statues of naked men on pedestals, and tall mahogany bookshelves. The scent of old parchment hangs thick in the air, and the walls are lined with tall shelves packed with colorful, leather-bound books.
event_triggers: On search (DC 16 Arcana): One scroll contains the true history of Manshoon's clones -- there have been dozens, and they have spent centuries killing each other.

content_hints.description: The shop T2. The Bent Nail contains displays of ornate wooden furniture, bows, crossbows, finely carved wooden canes, quarterstaffs, and shields. The owner, Tally Fellbranch, sells wooden weapons and shields at normal cost and also crafts and sells furniture and wood sculptures.
content_hints.read_aloud: > You see a space where the air hangs heavy, thick with the scent of aged parchment and something faintly metallic. Rows upon rows of legal tomes and financial ledgers stack high atop a solid mahogany desk, and a freestanding suit of bronze armor stands sentinel just inside the door.

hazards.name: Leaking alchemical fluid (acid damage on contact)

event_triggers: On disturbance: The failed clone awakens -- a half-formed thing with Manshoon's face and none of his mind. It attacks anything that moves.

content_hints.description: Smoke and steam billow from the many windows around this indoor forge where metal weapons, armor, and tools are made. The forge is owned and operated by the fire genasi Embric and the water genasi Avi, both members of the Most Careful Order of Skilled Smiths and Metalforgers.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a sand table filling the room, its surface sculpted into a detailed relief map of a great city—you recognize the harbor, the castle, the tangled streets of the Dock Ward. Tiny figurines are positioned across it in clusters: black figures in the south, purple in the east, silver scattered throughout, and a single golden piece placed deep beneath the city's center. The figurines have been moved recently; you can see the drag marks in the sand. Someone is playing a very large game.
event_triggers: On study (DC 14 History or Investigation): The map reveals the approximate location of the Vault of Dragons and Manshoon's planned assault route.

content_hints.description: The Xanathar Guild has hideouts throughout Waterdeep’s sewers, and the floor plan of this locale can be reused for other hideouts. The boss of this hideout is a half-orc named Grum’shar, who is interrogating Floon Blagmaar in area Q7.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a chamber carved from black marble, its walls etched with constellations you do not recognize. The curved floor meets curved walls meets curved ceiling in a seamless whole that makes your sense of gravity uncertain. At the center, a figure floats in still air, cross-legged, surrounded by orbiting books that turn their own pages in a slow, silent carousel.

loot.name: Robe of the Archmagi (cursed -- bears Manshoon's sigil)

hazards.name: Contingency spell (triggers Shield on first attack)

event_triggers: On combat start: 'You have come to kill me. Others have tried. I have died before -- it is surprisingly educational. Shall we begin?'
On Manshoon at half HP: He gestures and the ceiling becomes transparent, revealing the night sky above Waterdeep. 'This city will be mine. If not this body, then the next. I am patient.'
On defeat: Manshoon's body dissolves. Somewhere in another hidden sanctum, a clone's eyes open.

content_hints.description: The library fills two floors of this three-story building, and it somehow seems to contain more shelves than the building should be able to hold. The scent of old books permeates the air.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see the front of the bookstore adorned with a charming sign of a gold dragon curled around a treasure hoard of books and scrolls. Inside, the shop is decorated with beautiful hardwood, and the earthy scent of old books permeates the air.

hazards.name: Symbol of Death on vault interior

event_triggers: On soul coins examined: Each coin contains a screaming face pressed against the interior surface. They are warm to the touch.
